_note: ROUND 2, batch b_1000BC_0_1 (R2A_ prefix) — one more documented generation behind the round-1 spouse-parents. Web-verified July 2026 against Wikipedia/Wikidata for each figure named below plus the cited ancient sources. NEVER re-parents an existing node: PTOLEMY5, CLEOPATRA1, ANTIOCHUS2, LAODICE1, ANTIPATER, CYPROSNAB, HERODGREAT, MARIAMNE, ROM_CATO_ELDER and SPC_LIVIADRUSA are all referenced by id only; where an existing pair already stands, the SAME two parent ids are re-stated so the build only APPENDS a sibling. FILLED (documented): SPC_ARIARATHES3 <- Ariaramnes of Cappadocia <- Ariarathes II (Cappadocian king-list, Diodorus/Wikipedia). SPC_STRATONICE_CAP <- the EXISTING Seleucid pair ANTIOCHUS2 + LAODICE1 (she is their attested daughter, sister of Seleucus II — Wikipedia 'Stratonice of Cappadocia'). SPC_RUTILIA <- Publius Rutilius, whose other children were P. Rutilius Rufus (cos 105 BC) & Lucius (Wikipedia 'Rutilia gens'/'Aurelia (mother of Caesar)'). SPI_PTOLEMY6 & SPI_CLEOPATRA2 <- the EXISTING pair PTOLEMY5 + CLEOPATRA1 (both already stand as parents of PTOLEMY8 in 03_antiquity.json; this appends their other two attested children). SPC_LICINIA_MUC <- L. Licinius Crassus 'the Orator' (cos 95 BC) — a DIFFERENT Lucius Licinius Crassus from the existing SPC_LLICINIUSCRASSUS (son of P. Licinius Crassus cos 97, d.87 BC), flagged as distinct in-label. SPC_LIVIADRUSA <- M. Livius Drusus (cos 112 BC) & Cornelia, gaining documented siblings the reformer M. Livius Drusus (tribune 91 BC) and Mamercus Aemilius Lepidus Livianus (cos 77 BC, adopted into the Aemilii). SPC_JULIAANTONIA <- L. Julius Caesar (cos 90 BC) <- L. Julius Caesar (praetor 94 BC) & Fulvia, two clean generations per Wikipedia 'Lucius Julius Caesar (consul 90 BC)'; gains a documented brother, L. Julius Caesar (cos 64 BC) — his own Fulvia is NOT the same person as Mark Antony's later wife Fulvia, flagged in-label. SPC_MPORCIUSCATO_F <- M. Porcius Cato Salonianus <- the EXISTING ROM_CATO_ELDER by his second wife Salonia (Plutarch/Wikipedia 'Cato the Younger'), giving Cato the Younger's father his real place as Cato the Elder's grandson. SPC_POMPEIUSSTRABO <- Sextus Pompeius <- Gnaeus Pompeius, of the gens Pompeia of Picenum (Wikipedia 'Gnaeus Pompeius Strabo'). SPC_QMUCIUSSCAEVOLA <- Publius Mucius Scaevola (cos 133 BC, Pontifex Maximus) — Wikipedia 'Quintus Mucius Scaevola Pontifex'. SPC_AUFIDIA <- Aufidius Lurco, named in her own round-1 label (Suetonius, Tiberius 3). SPC_MANTONIUSCRETICUS <- Marcus Antonius 'the Orator' (cos 99 BC, d.87 BC), named in his own round-1 label. SPI_SALOME1 <- the EXISTING pair ANTIPATER + CYPROSNAB (Herod the Great's own parents, already a union in 07_biblical.json bearing HERODGREAT; this appends their attested daughter Salome I — Josephus). SPC_MTULLIUSCICERO_F <- M. Tullius Cicero (the orator's grandfather, 'a leading man of Arpinum') & Gratidia, sister of M. Gratidius (Plutarch, Cicero 1). SPC_SERVILIA <- Q. Servilius Caepio (praetor 91 BC) & the EXISTING SPC_LIVIADRUSA (her earlier marriage, already noted in her round-1 label), gaining documented siblings a younger Servilia and Gnaeus Servilius Caepio (Wikipedia 'Servilia (mother of Brutus)'). SPK_SALAMPSIO <- the EXISTING pair HERODGREAT + MARIAMNE (already a union bearing ARISTOBULUS4; this appends their attested daughter Salampsio — Josephus). SKIPPED (undocumented further ancestry, reported separately): SPB_ANDIA, SPB_NICOMACHUS_STA (only a legendary centuries-spanning Asclepiad descent claim via Machaon, not a named immediate parent), SPB_PHAESTIS, SPB_ANTIOCHUS_GEN, SPB_LAODICE_SEL, SPC_LAURELIUSCOTTA, SPC_HELVIA, SPC_LUCILIA, SPC_ANNIA_CINNA, SPC_MFULVIUSBAMBALIO, SPC_SEMPRONIA_FUL, SPC_LCORNELIUSCINNA (modern sources call the link to the cos-127-BC Cinna 'not certain'), SPC_MJUNIUSBRUTUSELDER, SPC_DJUNIUSSILANUS ('may have been' son of M. Junius Silanus cos 109 — too hedged), SPC_MLIVIUSDRUSUSCL (Borghesi's two competing candidate fathers, no single attested one). No fabrication — unnamed mothers/fathers drawn as '—' placeholders, never invented; ordered so every new id is defined before it is referenced.

families.note: CAPPADOCIA — the standard Ariarathid king-list one generation further back: Ariarathes II, father of Ariaramnes.

a.label:
👑 Ariarathes II of Cappadocia
c.280 BC · satrap-turned-king of Cappadocia; father of Ariaramnes · historical

children.label:
👑 Ariaramnes of Cappadocia
c.280-230 BC · king of Cappadocia; father of Ariarathes III · historical

families.note: CAPPADOCIA — Ariaramnes fathers the existing Ariarathes III, first of the house to take the royal title.

SELEUCID EXTENSION — appended to the EXISTING pair Antiochus II Theos & Laodice I (both referenced by id; same parents re-stated). Stratonice of Cappadocia is their attested daughter, full sister of Seleucus II Callinicus (already the pair's child in 03_antiquity.json).

ROME — Rutilia's father & siblings, per Wikipedia 'Rutilia gens'/'Aurelia (mother of Caesar)': Rutilia was one of three children of a Publius Rutilius, her brothers being P. Rutilius Rufus (the jurist-historian, consul 105 BC) and Lucius.

a.label:
Publius Rutilius
c.175 BC · Roman senator of the gens Rutilia; father of Rutilia, P. Rutilius Rufus & Lucius · historical

children.label:
Publius Rutilius Rufus
c.158-c.78 BC · consul 105 BC, jurist & historian; unjustly exiled in 92 BC, wrote his memoirs abroad; brother of Rutilia · historical

Lucius Rutilius
c.165 BC · brother of Rutilia & of P. Rutilius Rufus · historical

families.note: PTOLEMAIC EXTENSION — appended to the EXISTING pair Ptolemy V Epiphanes & Cleopatra I 'Syra' (both referenced by id; same parents re-stated as their existing child Ptolemy VIII in 03_antiquity.json). Ptolemy VI Philometor & Cleopatra II were their other two attested children.

ROME — Licinia, wife of Q. Mucius Scaevola Pontifex, was daughter of L. Licinius Crassus 'the Orator' (cos 95 BC), Cicero's model in De Oratore. He is DISTINCT from the existing SPC_LLICINIUSCRASSUS (son of the consul P. Licinius Crassus, d.87 BC in the Marian purge) — a different man of the same name, flagged in-label.

a.label:
Lucius Licinius Crassus 'the Orator'
140-91 BC · consul 95 BC, the great advocate of Cicero's De Oratore (distinct from the younger L. Licinius Crassus, son of consul P. Licinius Crassus, already in the tree); father of Licinia · historical

families.note: ROME — Livia (Drusa)'s parents & siblings: M. Livius Drusus (cos 112 BC, censor 109 BC, died in office) & Cornelia. Their other children were M. Livius Drusus 'the reformer' (tribune 91 BC, whose assassination triggered the Social War) and Mamercus (adopted into the Aemilii Lepidi). Wikipedia 'Marcus Livius Drusus (consul)'/'(reformer)'.

a.label:
Marcus Livius Drusus
c.155-108(d.in office) BC · consul 112 BC & censor 109 BC; father of the reformer, of Livia (Drusa) & of Mamercus · historical

b.label:
Cornelia
c.155 BC · wife of M. Livius Drusus (cos 112 BC); mother of the reformer, Livia (Drusa) & Mamercus · historical

children.label:
Marcus Livius Drusus 'the reformer'
d.91 BC · tribune of the plebs 91 BC; his reform program & assassination triggered the Social War; brother of Livia (Drusa) · historical

Mamercus Aemilius Lepidus Livianus
c.118 BC · born a Livius Drusus, adopted into the Aemilii Lepidi; consul 77 BC; married a daughter of Sulla; brother of Livia (Drusa) · historical

families.note: ROME — the Julii Caesares one generation further back: L. Julius Caesar (cos 90 BC, Julia's father) was himself son of L. Julius Caesar (praetor 94 BC, propraetor of Macedonia) & Fulvia. Wikipedia 'Lucius Julius Caesar (consul 90 BC)'. This Fulvia is NOT the later Fulvia who married Mark Antony (flagged to avoid confusion).

a.label:
Lucius Julius Caesar
c.170 BC · praetor 94 BC & propraetor of Macedonia; father of L. Julius Caesar (cos 90 BC) · historical

b.label:
Fulvia
c.165 BC · wife of L. Julius Caesar (praetor 94 BC) — distinct from the later Fulvia who married Mark Antony; mother of L. Julius Caesar (cos 90 BC) · historical

children.label:
Lucius Julius Caesar
c.134-87 BC · consul 90 BC in the Social War; second cousin of Julius Caesar's father; father of Julia (mother of Mark Antony) · historical

families.note: ROME — L. Julius Caesar (cos 90 BC) fathers the existing Julia (Mark Antony's mother) and her documented brother L. Julius Caesar (cos 64 BC).

children.label:
Lucius Julius Caesar
c.100-46 BC · consul 64 BC; brother of Julia, mother of Mark Antony · historical

families.note: ROME — the EXISTING Cato the Elder (ROM_CATO_ELDER, standalone in 29b_punic_qin.json) gains a SECOND, real union with his attested second wife Salonia, bearing M. Porcius Cato Salonianus. This does not re-parent Cato the Elder; it adds his true second marriage per Plutarch/Wikipedia 'Cato the Younger'.

b.label:
Salonia
c.175 BC · second wife of Cato the Elder, married in his old age; mother of M. Porcius Cato Salonianus · Plutarch, Cato the Elder 24

children.label:
Marcus Porcius Cato Salonianus
c.154 BC · son of Cato the Elder by his second wife Salonia; father of M. Porcius Cato (Cato the Younger's father) · historical

families.note: ROME — M. Porcius Cato Salonianus fathers the existing M. Porcius Cato, making Cato the Younger the true grandson of Cato the Elder.
ROME — Gnaeus Pompeius Strabo's own father & grandfather, of the gens Pompeia of Picenum. Wikipedia 'Gnaeus Pompeius Strabo'.

a.label:
Gnaeus Pompeius
c.190 BC · of the gens Pompeia of Picenum; father of Sextus Pompeius · historical

children.label:
Sextus Pompeius
c.165 BC · of Picenum; father of Gnaeus Pompeius Strabo · historical

families.note: ROME — Sextus Pompeius fathers the existing Gnaeus Pompeius Strabo, Pompey the Great's father.
ROME — Q. Mucius Scaevola 'Pontifex' was son of P. Mucius Scaevola (cos 133 BC), also Pontifex Maximus. Wikipedia 'Quintus Mucius Scaevola Pontifex'/'Publius Mucius Scaevola (consul 133 BC)'.

a.label:
Publius Mucius Scaevola
c.176-c.115 BC · consul 133 BC & Pontifex Maximus, the jurist; father of Q. Mucius Scaevola 'Pontifex' · historical

families.note: ROME — Aufidia's father, named in her own round-1 label. Suetonius, Tiberius 3.

a.label:
Aufidius Lurco
1st c. BC · Roman eques; father of Aufidia, grandfather of the empress Livia · Suetonius, Tiberius 3

families.note: ROME — M. Antonius Creticus's father, named in his own round-1 label: the orator Marcus Antonius, killed in the Marian proscriptions of 87 BC.

a.label:
Marcus Antonius 'the Orator'
143-87 BC · consul 99 BC, the leading Roman orator of his generation before Cicero; killed in the Marian proscriptions; father of M. Antonius Creticus, grandfather of Mark Antony · historical

families.note: HERODIAN — appended to the EXISTING pair Antipater the Idumaean & Cypros (both referenced by id; their existing union already bears HERODGREAT in 07_biblical.json). Salome I, Herod's sister, is their attested daughter. Josephus, Antiquities.

ROME — Cicero's paternal grandfather & grandmother: M. Tullius Cicero, 'a leading man of Arpinum' who petitioned in 115 BC for the extension of the secret ballot, married Gratidia, sister of M. Gratidius. Plutarch, Cicero 1.

a.label:
Marcus Tullius Cicero
c.170 BC · a leading man of Arpinum, grandfather of the orator Cicero · Plutarch, Cicero 1

b.label:
Gratidia
c.165 BC · sister of Marcus Gratidius; wife of M. Tullius Cicero (the elder); grandmother of the orator Cicero · Plutarch, Cicero 1

families.note: ROME — Servilia's father Q. Servilius Caepio (praetor 91 BC) & the EXISTING Livia (Drusa) — her earlier marriage, already noted in Livia's own round-1 label. Their other attested children were a younger Servilia and Gnaeus Servilius Caepio. Wikipedia 'Servilia (mother of Brutus)'.

a.label:
Quintus Servilius Caepio
c.130-90 BC · quaestor 100 BC & praetor 91 BC; first husband of Livia (Drusa); father of Servilia, mother of Brutus · historical

children.label:
Servilia
c.98 BC · younger daughter of Q. Servilius Caepio & Livia (Drusa); sister of Servilia, mother of Brutus · historical

Gnaeus Servilius Caepio
c.96 BC · son of Q. Servilius Caepio & Livia (Drusa); brother of Servilia, mother of Brutus · historical

families.note: HERODIAN — appended to the EXISTING pair Herod the Great & Mariamne I (both referenced by id; their existing union already bears ARISTOBULUS4 in 03_antiquity.json). Salampsio is their attested daughter. Josephus, Antiquities.
